mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month decreases in FEDFUNDS do not correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns; walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS tighten financial conditions sufficiently to force de-risking in equity portfolios, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal accelerating labor market tightness that boosts consumption expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises MoM and UNRATE falls MoM
failure_condition: In months where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or the hit rate is below 50% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, month-over-month declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) signal weakening consumer demand that compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is above 280 and RSAFS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of instances or the Sharpe ratio is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns; walk-forward hit rate remains below 52% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window triggers equity market repricing of recession risk, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and declines in housing starts (HOUST) transmit restrictive monetary policy into the interest-rate-sensitive real economy, amplifying equity risk premia and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises MoM and HOUST falls MoM
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of rising FEDFUNDS and falling HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns; walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals expanding aggregate demand that supports broad corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not positively predict 60-day S&P 500 returns; walk-forward hit rate remains below 52% over any 10-year rolling window.
